title: Issue tree builder
prompt: Break the problem into three clean branches.
problemStatement: Why is the Olympics losing its cultural impact?

sampleAnswer.hypothesis: The Olympics is losing gravity because of broken culture, a lost story arc, and lack of legitimate transparency.

arguments.title: Shared ritual is breaking
supportingDetails: Fragmented attention: no single shared moment; audiences split across platforms and time zones.
Broadcast-first packaging: still feels like a TV event while audiences consume through feeds.
Highlights over attendance: people catch up later, so the Games lose the live collective feeling.

arguments.title: Meaning is getting replaced by moment-chasing
supportingDetails: Clip culture: short-form rewards shock and spectacle; meaning needs arcs and buildup.
Virality outshines excellence — so sacredness doesn't accumulate over time.
Winners aren't legible: without athlete-led arcs, audiences don't feel the personal stakes.

arguments.title: Legitimacy and tone are getting noisier
supportingDetails: Trust volatility: politics and polarization make the Olympics feel biased depending on the viewer.
Generic unity messaging lands as tone-deaf in a fractured world.
Over-commercialization signal: sponsorship load makes the Games feel sold, not earned.
